contradiction_text: The tailored interface improves benchmark performance versus shell-only and retrieval-augmented setups, yet full SWE-bench success remains low in absolute terms.
related_claim_texts: SWE-agent with GPT-4 Turbo achieved a substantially higher resolve rate on SWE-bench than the prior reported non-interactive retrieval-augmented baseline.
The paper’s evidence supports a bounded mechanism claim that SWE-agent’s interface design helps performance by structuring search and editing with constrained feedback and lint-guarded edits.
disclosure: The performance gain over prior baselines should stay visible, but so should the fact that the system still solves only a small minority of full SWE-bench tasks.

contradiction_text: The interface’s editing guardrails improve results, but editing is also a major failure path, with failed edits common and strongly associated with worse outcomes.
related_claim_texts: The paper’s evidence supports a bounded mechanism claim that SWE-agent’s interface design helps performance by structuring search and editing with constrained feedback and lint-guarded edits.
SWE-agent’s gains are materially limited by editing instability, with failed edits common and strongly associated with lower eventual task success.
disclosure: The article should keep visible that lint-guarded editing helps on the benchmark, while the same editing subsystem still breaks often enough to remain a central unresolved bottleneck.

contradiction_text: Search structure appears helpful, but not all added search interaction helps; iterative search performs worse than both summarized search and no-search.
related_claim_texts: The paper’s evidence supports a bounded mechanism claim that SWE-agent’s interface design helps performance by structuring search and editing with constrained feedback and lint-guarded edits.
disclosure: The article should not collapse this into a generic claim that more search tooling is always better; the visible conclusion is narrower, namely that interface quality and feedback format matter.

contradiction_text: The interface transfers to another model, but the transferred results are weaker than the GPT-4 Turbo configuration, so portability does not imply model-agnostic performance.
related_claim_texts: The paper’s evidence supports a bounded mechanism claim that SWE-agent’s interface design helps performance by structuring search and editing with constrained feedback and lint-guarded edits.
disclosure: The article should keep the portability claim visible as real but limited; the same interface does not erase backend-model performance differences.
